nodes.summary: Abort on dirty working tree with non-zero exit and human-readable message
Abort when BLUEPRINT_UPGRADE_REF is unset or does not resolve
Abort when blueprint/contract.yaml is absent, unparseable, or wrong repo_mode
Invoke apply with BLUEPRINT_UPGRADE_ALLOW_DELETE set to pipeline default; flag overridable and documented
Contract resolver preserves consumer identity fields: name, repo_mode, description
Contract resolver merges required_files additively; drops consumer-added entries with missing files
Contract resolver drops prune globs matching existing consumer paths; records in decision report
Contract resolver emits structured JSON decision report to artifacts/blueprint/contract_resolve_decisions.json
Pipeline compares all contract-referenced files against disk after Stage 2 apply
Fetch absent contract-referenced files from BLUEPRINT_UPGRADE_SOURCE via local git; no HTTP
Overwrite bootstrap template mirror when workspace file under scripts/templates/blueprint/bootstrap/<path> exists
Scan new/changed markdown for make <target> references; warn on missing .PHONY; do not abort
Run quality-docs-sync-generated-reference exactly once after all file mutations
Run gate chain (infra-validate then quality-hooks-run); abort on first failure with structured error
Emit residual report to artifacts/blueprint/upgrade-residual.md always, even on partial failure
Every residual report item MUST include a prescribed action (no free-form notes)
Residual report lists consumer_owned_manual_review files with prescribed actions
Residual report lists consumer test paths absent from test_pyramid_contract.json
Existing individual make targets remain independently callable with unchanged behavior
No external HTTP fetches; Stage 5 file retrieval via local git only
Idempotent: twice on clean working tree after successful first run → no changes, exit 0
No consumer-specific logic in pipeline scripts; all config read from blueprint/contract.yaml
Emit stage-labeled structured progress lines to stdout before and after each stage
make blueprint-upgrade-consumer BLUEPRINT_UPGRADE_REF=vX.Y.Z runs end-to-end and exits 0 on clean tree
contract.yaml name and repo_mode preserved through upgrade; verified by unit test on fixture conflict JSON
Absent required file auto-fetched from upgrade source; verified by integration test against minimal fixture
Superseded action=skip files deleted by Stage 2; verified by existing blueprint-upgrade-fresh-env-gate
Residual report always generated (even on partial failure) with every item having a prescribed action
Existing upgrade gate tests pass without modification
